title: Tapping for the Windfall Fantasy
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing the habit of waiting to be rescued by a large, unexpected sum of money, and grounding in the wealth that comes through consistent work
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the pull toward windfall thinking — lottery wins, inheritances, a sudden influx that changes everything overnight. The script works with the scarcity signal underneath the fantasy, and moves toward the real wealth that patient, consistent work builds.
sourceNotes: Original tapping script written for homemade.education. Uses Gary Craig's EFT framework (mid-1990s). Related principles from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024) and MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice the windfall fantasy surfacing — when your plans hinge on a large, unlikely sum arriving, or when you feel more pulled toward lottery thinking than toward building steadily.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the windfall fantasy — the recurring hope of a large, unexpected sum that will fix everything. The script does not shame the fantasy. It reads it as the signal it is: a message that something underneath needs attention. The practice works with that signal and moves toward the real, unglamorous wealth that consistent work produces.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though part of me wants the windfall to come and solve everything, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I sometimes wish money would arrive without me having to build it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though the fantasy feels more certain than the work, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: the fantasy is telling me something I want.
Side of eye: I can want that thing without the lottery being the only path.
Under eye: rescue thinking means I don't yet trust the work.
Under nose: the work is what actually builds lasting wealth.
Chin: I am willing to trust the slow path.
Collarbone: I release the waiting-to-be-rescued pattern.
Under arm: I put my faith in what I can build with my own hands.
Top of head: my wealth comes through me, not to me by accident.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I trust consistent effort to compound into real wealth.
Side of eye: I build something no windfall could give me: confidence.
Under eye: I know how money works because I have built it myself.
Under nose: the unglamorous work is the real prize.
Chin: I choose to trust what I can create.
Collarbone: I am the source of my own financial shift.
Under arm: I stop waiting and start building.
Top of head: the wealth I build is mine to keep.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

Written for homemade.education. Uses Gary Craig's EFT tapping framework, in use since the mid-1990s. Related principles from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024) and MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
